patches.find: The best result (6.2% RMSE) is still 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-meter accuracy.
patches.replace: The best result (6.2% RMSE) is still 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-centimeter accuracy.
patches.rationale: WIN-057.c4 hole #3 (minor): Vincenty/WGS84 achieves sub-millimeter to sub-centimeter accuracy, not sub-meter. 'Sub-meter' understates the geodesy advantage by 2-3 orders of magnitude. The verdict body already correctly says 'sub-0.01% via Vincenty'. Single-word fix for consistency.

patches.find: with a region of explicit non-applicability carved out around the equator (OPEN-015). Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is in-sample retrodiction, not prediction.
patches.replace: with a region of explicit non-applicability carved out around the equator (OPEN-015). The iterative-refinement framing (V13 is at the Clarke 1866 stage) misses that early geodetic models (Clarke 1866, Bessel 1841) reached <0.5% relative error on continental baselines without exclusion zones — the dome's V13 regresses when asked to cover a broader surface range. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is in-sample retrodiction, not prediction.
patches.rationale: WIN-057.c4 hole #2 (moderate) + preemptive recommendation: add Clarke/Bessel comparison to preempt the 'V13 is at the Clarke 1866 stage' iterative-refinement defense. Early geodetic models (Clarke 1866, Bessel 1841) reached <0.5% on continental baselines without exclusion zones, showing that the dome's exclusion requirement is not a normal stage of geodetic refinement but a regression. Curmudgeon defense_survives=2 on this defense; the sentence directly addresses it.
